questions.question: What is the canonical navigation path to reach the Shopify orders list in FIMS?

options.text: Online → Shopify → Orders
Dashboard → Orders → Shopify
OMS → Shopify → Orders
Support → OMS → Orders

questions.question: A CRM Executive sees an order in the Follow Up tab in FIMS. What does this indicate?

options.text: The order has been confirmed and is ready for packing
The order has been permanently cancelled and needs no further action
The order requires CRM action and must be addressed the same day
The order is in transit and needs monitoring only

questions.question: An order in FIMS displays a red SLA Breached tag. What is the correct response?

options.text: Flag the order for the CRM Asst Manager and continue processing new orders first
Cancel the order — SLA Breached means the delivery window has expired
Action the SLA Breached order immediately, above all other orders in the current queue
Move the order to the Claims tab and escalate to the Asst Manager

questions.question: A COD customer answers the call and verbally confirms their order. The address on file is complete and verified. Which action button is correct?

options.text: Auto Confirmed
Self Approved
Register Communication
Confirmation Call (Confirmed)

questions.question: For which of the following COD scenarios should Auto Confirmed be selected instead of Confirmation Call (Confirmed)?

options.text: Customer verbally confirmed on phone call; address is complete
Customer confirmed via WhatsApp message; address is complete and traceable
Customer did not respond to the call or WhatsApp message
Customer requested to cancel the order after confirming verbally

questions.question: A COD customer sends a WhatsApp confirmation, but the address on file has no flat number. What is the correct action?

options.text: Auto Confirmed — the customer has already confirmed via WhatsApp
Cancellation Request — address issues always require cancellation
Call Attempted — contact the customer to verify and correct the address
Convert to Self Pickup — incomplete address means the customer must collect

questions.question: A CRM Executive calls a COD customer three times and gets no answer. What is the correct action sequence?

options.text: Cancel the order immediately after the third unanswered call
Select Auto Confirmed — an unreachable COD customer is treated as implicit confirmation
Select Call Attempted → Initiate WhatsApp Flow
Select Self Approved and move the order to To Pack

questions.question: A Prepaid order arrives. The CRM Executive calls the customer and gets no answer. The address on file is complete and traceable. What is the correct action?

options.text: Call Attempted — wait for the customer to call back before confirming
Auto Confirmed → To Pack, with remark: Prepaid order – address verified, customer not reachable
Cancellation Request — an unreachable customer cannot confirm delivery intent
Initiate WhatsApp Flow — confirmation call is required for all orders

questions.question: Which statement correctly describes the address verification requirement for Prepaid orders?

options.text: Address verification is not required — payment is already received, so delivery is attempted regardless
Address verification is mandatory for Prepaid orders, even though a verbal confirmation call is not required
Address verification is only required if the pincode falls outside the courier's serviceable area
Address verification is handled by the courier partner, not by CRM

questions.question: A Prepaid order has an incomplete address. The CRM Executive calls the customer and gets no answer. What is the correct action?

options.text: Auto Confirmed — the payment is received; attempt delivery with the partial address
Call Attempted → Initiate WhatsApp Flow → wait for customer response before confirming
Cancellation Request — incomplete address means the order cannot be delivered
Convert to Self Pickup — the customer must collect since address cannot be verified remotely

questions.question: Why must a CRM Executive always review the Order History in FIMS before taking any action on an order?

options.text: To verify the customer's phone number before making a confirmation call
To check if another team member has already actioned the order, preventing duplicate entries or conflicting status updates
To confirm the product name and SKU before writing the remark
Order History review is required only for SLA Breached orders, not for all orders

questions.question: After selecting Call Attempted on a COD order where the customer did not answer and a WhatsApp message was sent, which remark is correct?

options.text: COD order – no response received
Customer unreachable – order pending confirmation
WhatsApp sent – confirmation awaited
Call attempt – no response, WhatsApp message sent

questions.question: A CRM Executive clicks Auto Confirmed on a Prepaid order and immediately navigates to the next order without adding a remark. Which rule has been violated?

options.text: The FIFO processing rule — the Executive must complete all steps on one order before moving to the next
The KRA 4 SOP Adherence rule — a remark is mandatory after every action and this is audited
The escalation protocol — Auto Confirmed orders require Asst Manager review before processing
No rule has been violated — remarks are optional for Prepaid orders

questions.question: A COD order is confirmed by the customer via WhatsApp and the address is fully verified. What is the correct remark to add?

options.text: COD confirmed – WhatsApp verification received
Auto Confirmed – WhatsApp reply received
Customer confirmed on WhatsApp – address verified
WhatsApp confirmation noted – order dispatched

questions.question: A CRM Executive opens FIMS and finds the following orders: 4 new COD orders, 1 overdue COD order, and 1 SLA Breached order. In what order should these be processed?

options.text: New COD orders → Overdue COD → SLA Breached (red tags are flagged for manager attention)
SLA Breached → Overdue COD → New COD orders
Overdue COD → New COD orders → SLA Breached
Process all orders in the order they appear in the FIMS order list, top to bottom

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the FIFO processing rule as applied in FIMS order management?

options.text: Process the most recently added orders first, regardless of SLA or urgency
Process the oldest orders first within each priority tier, with SLA Breached orders always processed before all others
Process all orders in creation order without any exceptions for SLA or overdue status
The FIFO rule applies only to Prepaid orders; COD orders are processed based on call-attempt availability
